macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Liquidations Surge to $60M in 24 Hours with Shorts Dominating Losses
items.summary: Total crypto liquidations reached $59.82 million over the past 24 hours, with short positions accounting for $38.93 million compared to $20.89 million in longs; Bitcoin and Ethereum saw significantly higher short liquidations.

items.title: Bitcoin Trades Around $67,000 Amid Bearish Sentiment and Failed Safe-Haven Narrative
items.summary: Bitcoin hovered near $67,000 after declining from $70,000, with bearish sentiment rising and its safe-haven status questioned amid geopolitical tensions and oil price surges.

items.title: XRP Rises 3.23% to $1.35 on Ripple Partnership and ETF Buzz
items.summary: XRP gained 3.23% amid optimism from Ripple’s Convera partnership and regulatory progress; SEC acknowledged Canary’s spot XRP ETF filing, potentially launching soon.

items.title: Block Launches Free Bitcoin Faucet After 16 Years
items.summary: Jack Dorsey's Block announced a free Bitcoin faucet, reviving the promotional tool and stirring optimism amid market downturns.

items.title: Pi Network's PI Token Stabilizes Above $0.17 After Losses
items.summary: PI token held above $0.17 following a sharp decline, while VeChain climbed 9% and HASH dropped 10% in notable altcoin moves.

items.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum Steady Despite Trump’s Iran Ultimatum
items.summary: BTC at $67,404 and ETH at $2,073 saw slight 1% gains as markets awaited Middle East developments after President Trump’s 48-hour warning to Iran.

items.title: FLOKI Surges on Elon Musk’s 'Dog CEO' Revival
items.summary: Floki meme coin rallied double digits after Elon Musk referenced his viral 'dog CEO' joke.

items.title: High Volume Altcoins: AriaAI Up 15.82%, JuChain +2.24% with Massive Gains
items.summary: Top volume cryptos included AriaAI (+15.82% to $0.56) and JuChain (+2.24% amid 84% weekly surge), per live rankings.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Undergoes Major C-Suite Overhaul
items.summary: OpenAI is experiencing significant leadership changes with COO Brad Lightcap shifting out of his role and Fidji Simo, the AGI CEO, taking a medical leave of absence, signaling another round of executive restructuring at the company.

items.title: Meta Pauses Mercor Collaboration Following Data Breach
items.summary: Meta has halted its work with AI training startup Mercor after a cyberattack exposed sensitive AI industry data, raising concerns about security in the AI development ecosystem.

items.title: Luma AI Raises $900 Million at $4 Billion Valuation
items.summary: Video generation platform Luma AI secured $900 million in funding, bringing its valuation to approximately $4 billion, with CEO Amit Jain emphasizing the capital-intensive nature of the domain.

items.title: AMD, Cisco, and Saudi's Humain Launch AI Joint Venture
items.summary: A new joint venture between AMD, Cisco, and Saudi Arabia's Humain will launch with a 100-megawatt data center project in Saudi Arabia, with Luma AI contracted as the first major customer.

items.title: Google Opens Largest AI Infrastructure Center Outside US in Taiwan
items.summary: Alphabet's Google inaugurated its largest artificial intelligence infrastructure hardware engineering centre outside the United States in Taiwan, demonstrating long-term commitment to the region.

items.title: Nvidia and Menlo Micro Accelerate AI Chip Testing by 30-90%
items.summary: Nvidia and Menlo Micro developed new switching chips that significantly speed up AI-chip testing, addressing a major production bottleneck as demand for AI chips surges.

items.title: IBM Expands FedRAMP Authorization for AI Solutions
items.summary: IBM announced that 11 of its AI and automation software solutions, including products from the watsonx portfolio, have received Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) authorization.

items.title: Moonbounce Raises $12 Million for AI Content Moderation
items.summary: Moonbounce secured $12 million in funding to expand its AI control engine that converts content moderation policies into consistent, predictable AI systems.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off Head of AI Division
items.summary: Take-Two, owner of Rockstar Games, laid off the head of its AI division along with an undisclosed number of staff members.

items.title: Toonstar Partners with HarperCollins to Create AI-Generated Digital Shows
items.summary: AI animation studio Toonstar is collaborating with HarperCollins to convert book franchises into digital shows using artificial intelligence technology.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Starfield PS5 Launch and Major Updates Announced
items.summary: Starfield is set to launch on PS5 on April 7th alongside a big free update called Free Lanes and a new paid DLC, Terran Armada. The updates include new locations and Unity changes.

items.title: Capcom Adds DRM to 30-Year-Old Resident Evil Ports
items.summary: Capcom has implemented Enigma DRM, described as an 'ice lock,' on 30-year-old Resident Evil games, affecting playability including on Steam Deck. The ports have seen some popularity despite criticism as lazy.

items.title: Legacy of Kain Series Faces New Setback
items.summary: Legacy of Kain has reportedly 'died again,' marking another challenge for the series amid ongoing development issues.

items.title: Former Bethesda Devs Criticize Studio Changes
items.summary: Ex-Bethesda developers discuss how growth from Fallout 4 to Starfield has compromised creative processes, work environment, and access to Todd Howard's leadership. They note ambitious but disappointing launches like Starfield.

items.title: Breath of Fire Series Revival Announced
items.summary: Falcom is bringing back the Breath of Fire RPG series, sparking excitement but concerns over the developer's bigger problems.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Microsoft Forces Windows 11 25H2 Installation on More Computers
items.summary: Microsoft is forcibly upgrading unmanaged Windows 11 24H2 PCs to version 25H2, expanding its automatic update rollout across more systems.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra Camera Bug Fixed
items.summary: Samsung confirmed a camera bug affecting the Galaxy S26 Ultra and released a fix to address the issue.

items.title: Google Pixel 11 Pro XL Renders Revealed
items.summary: CAD-based renders of the Google Pixel 11 Pro XL have leaked, showing the device's bold new camera bar design and completing the Pixel 11 family lineup.

items.title: Apple Releases iOS 26.5 Beta 1 for Developers
items.summary: Apple has released a new version of iOS 26.5 Beta 1 for developers, though no official release date has been announced.

items.title: Apple CarPlay Gains Google App Integration
items.summary: Apple CarPlay has received a significant update with integration of a major Google app before Android Auto.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Series Receives Deep Discounts
items.summary: The Samsung Galaxy S26, S26+, and S26 Ultra are all receiving significant price cuts in weekly deals.

items.title: iFixit Teardown: AirPods Max 2 Shows Same Design and Repairability Issues
items.summary: iFixit's teardown of the AirPods Max 2 reveals the device maintains the same design as its predecessor with unchanged repairability concerns.

items.title: NASA Solves Artemis II Microsoft Outlook Login Issues
items.summary: NASA successfully resolved Microsoft Outlook login glitches that affected the Artemis II mission, demonstrating the importance of IT support even for space operations.
